message.content: I want to update the copy on this website to be less focused exclusively on the Adironidacks and Capital district and to appear to a nation wide audience. I want to keep the framing on helping small businesses access the credit that they deserve and that we have a individual and personal connection with each of our clients and are not just a portal. Please review the copy on the home page suggest updates

file.content:
baseURL = "https://rexfordcommercialcapital.com/"
languageCode = "en-us"
title = "Rexford Commercial Capital"
enableRobotsTXT = true
canonifyURLs = false

[params]
  phone = "518-791-9771"
  phoneRaw = "15187919771"
  email = "info@rexfordcc.com"
  region = "Capital District & Adirondacks, NY"
  tagline = "Local Financing Expertise for Capital District & Adirondack Small Businesses"
  description = "Rexford Commercial Capital helps small businesses in the Capital District and Adirondacks secure commercial real estate, equipment, and working capital financing through local banks that know your community."
  formEndpoint = "https://rexford-inquiry-worker.joe5saia.workers.dev/inquiry"
  turnstileSiteKey = "0x4AAAAAAClwqB8h1a3thTcW"
  ga4ID = "G-WBKYLG69YV"
  bbbURL = "https://www.bbb.org/us/ny/rexford/profile/commercial-real-estate/rexford-commercial-capital-0041-236017820"

  [params.address]
    city = "Rexford"
    region = "NY"
    country = "US"
    zip = "12148"

[params.seo]
  ogImage = "/images/og-image.jpg"

[params.social]
  facebook = "https://www.facebook.com/rexfordcommercialcapital/"
  linkedin = "https://www.linkedin.com/company/rexford-commercial-capital/"

[markup]
  [markup.goldmark]
    [markup.goldmark.renderer]
      unsafe = false

[outputs]
  home = ["HTML", "RSS"]
  section = ["HTML", "RSS"]
  page = ["HTML"]

[menu]
  [[menu.main]]
    name = "Real Estate Financing"
    identifier = "real-estate"
    weight = 10
  [[menu.main]]
    name = "Fix & Flip"
    parent = "real-estate"
    url = "/services/fix-and-flip/"
    weight = 1
  [[menu.main]]
    name = "Rental Property Loans"
    parent = "real-estate"
    url = "/services/rental-property-loans/"
    weight = 2
  [[menu.main]]
    name = "Commercial Real Estate"
    parent = "real-estate"
    url = "/services/commercial-real-estate/"
    weight = 3
  [[menu.main]]
    name = "Bridge Loans"
    parent = "real-estate"
    url = "/services/bridge-loans/"
    weight = 4
  [[menu.main]]
    name = "Business Financing"
    identifier = "business"
    weight = 20
  [[menu.main]]
    name = "Equipment Financing"
    parent = "business"
    url = "/services/equipment-financing/"
    weight = 1
  [[menu.main]]
    name = "Business Line of Credit"
    parent = "business"
    url = "/services/business-line-of-credit/"
    weight = 2
  [[menu.main]]
    name = "Small Business Loans"
    parent = "business"
    url = "/services/small-business-loans/"
    weight = 3
  [[menu.main]]
    name = "Blog"
    url = "/blog/"
    weight = 30

content.thinking:
Now I have a full picture. Let me compile all the regional/local references that need to change and suggest updated copy. I want to:

1. Remove "Capital District & Adirondacks" specific references
2. Keep the personal/relationship-focused angle (Sarah and Craig, not a portal)
3. Keep "local" framing but broaden it to mean their personalized service approach rather than geographic limitation
4. Appeal to a nationwide small business audience

Let me go through each section:

**`content/_index.md`** (page meta description):
- Current: "Local financing expertise for Capital District & Adirondack small businesses."


- Suggested: "Expert small business financing — commercial real estate, equipment, and working capital — with the personal attention you deserve."

**`hugo.toml`** - `tagline` and `description` and `region`:
- tagline: "Local Financing Expertise for Capital District & Adirondack Small Businesses" → "Expert Financing for Small Businesses That Deserve Better"
- description: Remove regional focus, keep small business focus
- region: This is used in the footer as the address region (NY), that's okay

**`hero.html`**:
- Badge: "Capital District & Adirondacks Small Business Financing" → "Small Business Financing" with personalized service positioning in the headline and subheading instead of geographic claims.
- H1: "Local Financing. Local Relationships." → "Expert Financing. Personal Service." to emphasize expertise and attention rather than geography
- Body copy: Shifting away from regional language to focus on nationwide lending access and the core value of personalized broker service for commercial real estate, equipment, and working capital
- Trust bullets: Replacing "Capital District & Adirondacks" with "Access to 50+ Lenders" to highlight the breadth of financing options available

**`trust-bar.html`**:
- Removing geographic qualifiers like "local bank" and "Capital District & Adirondacks" in favor of "Deep Lender Relationships" and "Nationwide Small Business Financing" to reflect a broader service area

Now I'm updating the section leads across the site to remove regional references and emphasize the personal, direct relationship with Sarah and Craig, the breadth of transactions they've facilitated, and the outcomes they've helped clients achieve. The `hugo.toml` region parameter doesn't appear to be actively rendered, so I'll leave that for now.

For the hero section, "Local Financing. Local Relationships." feels geographically limiting, so I'm considering alternatives that keep the personal touch while broadening the appeal — "Small Business Financing. Real Relationships." feels like the strongest fit. The badge above the H1 should shift to something like "Commercial Financing for Small Businesses" to reinforce the focus without geographic constraints.

content.text:
Here's a full audit of the regional copy across the homepage, with suggested replacements. I've focused on keeping the "personal broker, not a portal" identity while dropping the geographic exclusivity.

---

## Copy Audit & Suggested Changes

### `content/_index.md` — Page meta description
**Current:** `Local financing expertise for Capital District & Adirondack small businesses.`
**Suggested:** `Expert small business financing — commercial real estate, equipment, and working capital — with the personal attention you deserve.`

---

### `hugo.toml` — Site tagline & description
**tagline (current):** `Local Financing Expertise for Capital District & Adirondack Small Businesses`
**Suggested:** `Expert Financing for Small Businesses That Deserve Better`

**description (current):** `...helps small businesses in the Capital District and Adirondacks secure...through local banks that know your community.`
**Suggested:** `Rexford Commercial Capital helps small businesses nationwide secure commercial real estate, equipment, and working capital financing — with the personal attention of a dedicated broker, not an online portal.`

---

### `hero.html` — Hero section

| Element | Current | Suggested |
|---|---|---|
| Badge (line 54) | `Capital District & Adirondacks Small Business Financing` | `Small Business Commercial Financing` |
| H1 (line 55) | `Local Financing. Local Relationships.` | `Expert Financing. Real Relationships.` |
| Body (line 56) | `...in the Capital District and Adirondacks...through local banks that know your community.` | `We help small businesses across the country secure commercial real estate, equipment, and working capital financing — with the personal attention a dedicated broker actually provides.` |
| Trust bullet (line 64) | `Capital District & Adirondacks` | `Nationwide Lender Network` |

---

### `trust-bar.html`

| Current | Suggested |
|---|---|
| `Deep Local Bank Relationships` | `Deep Lender Relationships` |
| `Capital District & Adirondacks` | `Nationwide Small Business Financing` |

---

### `team.html` — Section lead (line 4)
**Current:** `Sarah and Craig are based in the Capital District and personally handle every client relationship.`
**Suggested:** `Sarah and Craig personally handle every client relationship — you'll always speak directly with your broker, not a call center.`

---

### `recently-funded.html` — Section lead (line 4)
**Current:** `A sample of transactions we've recently facilitated for clients in the Capital District and Adirondacks.`
**Suggested:** `A sample of transactions we've recently facilitated for small business clients.`

---

### `testimonials.html` — Section lead (line 6)
**Current:** `Feedback from borrowers we have helped across the Capital District and Adirondacks.`
**Suggested:** `Feedback from small business owners we've helped access the financing they deserve.`

---

### Notes on what I'd leave as-is:
- The physical address in the footer (Rexford, NY) is fine — it's factual contact info, not a claim about service area.
- "Call Sarah or Craig" in the hero CTA is strong — it reinforces the personal angle, keep it.
- The "How It Works" and "CTA Band" sections have no regional references and read well as-is.

---

Want me to go ahead and apply all of these changes? I can do it in one pass across the relevant files.

content.text:
All 7 files updated. Here's a summary of what changed:

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `content/_index.md` | Page meta description — nationwide/personal framing |
| `hugo.toml` | Tagline and site description — removed all regional references |
| `hero.html` | Badge, H1, body copy, trust bullet — nationwide messaging |
| `trust-bar.html` | "Deep Local Bank Relationships" → "Deep Lender Relationships"; map-pin item → "Nationwide Small Business Financing" |
| `team.html` | Section lead — removed "Capital District", added "not a call center" |
| `recently-funded.html` | Section lead — removed regional qualifier |
| `testimonials.html` | Section lead — reframed around deserving credit access |

Run `make ci` to confirm the build is clean.
